turns.content: What brings you in today?
Well, doc, it's my knees. They ache something awful, especially first thing in the morning. I can barely get out of bed without letting out a groan.
When did this start?
Oh, it's been going on for a while now. I'd say maybe a year or so. It just keeps getting worse, little by little.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
Right in the front of both knees, mostly. It's like a deep, dull ache, not a sharp pain. Feels worse when I first get up, but once I move around a bit it eases up some.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, nothing like that. Just the knees.
What medications are you currently taking?
I take a little blue pill for my blood pressure, and one for my thyroid. I'm not sure what they're called, but I've been taking 'em for years.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
Yeah, the doctor told me I got high blood pressure and something wrong with my thyroid. That's about it, I think.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, not that I know of. I've never had a problem with any pills they gave me.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?
I just take a Tylenol now and then when it gets real bad. Sometimes I put a heating pad on 'em at night. Doesn't do much, though.
Does anything make it better or worse?
Well, getting moving helps a bit after that first stiffness in the morning. But cold weather or sitting too long makes 'em worse, that's for sure.
How has this been affecting your daily life?
It makes it hard to get around the house sometimes. I can't stand long at the stove like I used to, and going up the stairs takes me a while. It's just something I've learned to live with, I suppose.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
No, I never smoked. I might have a glass of wine at a wedding or something, but that's it. Not a regular drinker.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
I don't rightly know. My folks have passed on, and we never talked much about that kind of thing. I don't think so, though.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
No, not really. Just this past year or so. It started off as a little twinge, and now it's here all the time.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?
Yes, I think so. Whatever you think is best, doc.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Physically? It's probably a 7. Not the worst ever, but it wears you down day after day. Emotionally... maybe a 3 or 4. I'm not upset about it, just tired of dealing with it is all.
Do you have any questions for me?
No, I don't think so. You're the doctor — I trust you to tell me what needs to be done.
What do you think might be going on?
I really don't know, doc. That's why I came to you. I just know they hurt, and it's getting harder to ignore.
Is there anything else you think I should know?
No, I think that's about it. Just my knees acting up. Well, sometimes my hands feel stiff in the morning too, but that's probably just getting older.
